title: ModuleManifestV1
description: Schema for Hexalith frontend module manifests. Defines the module's identity, routes, and navigation entries. Source: packages/shell-api/src/manifest/manifestTypes.ts

manifestVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Always 1 for V1 manifests. Enables future schema evolution via discriminated union.

name.description: Module identifier in lowercase kebab-case. Used for routing, package naming, and directory names. Example: 'tenants', 'order-management'.

displayName.description: Human-readable module name shown in navigation and UI. Example: 'Tenant Management'.

version.description: Module version in semver format. Must match the module's package.json version. Example: '1.0.0'.

routes.description: Route definitions for the module. Each route is mounted under the module's base path. At least one route is required.

navigation.description: Navigation entries shown in the shell sidebar. Each entry's path should match a declared route.

migrationStatus.description: Optional migration status for modules transitioning from legacy systems. 'native' = built for this platform, 'coexisting' = runs alongside legacy, 'migrating' = actively being ported.

path.description: Route path starting with /. Mounted relative to the module's base path in the shell.

ModuleRoute.description: A route definition for the module.

label.description: Navigation label shown in the sidebar.

path.description: Navigation path. Must start with / and should match a declared route. Paths not matching a route produce a validation warning.

icon.description: Optional icon identifier for the navigation entry.

category.description: Optional category for grouping navigation entries in the sidebar.

ModuleNavigation.description: A navigation entry shown in the shell sidebar.
